title: THE LUCID HOUR
subtitle: Case 001
summary: Dr. Simon Vale dies during the Harlow Institute gala after apparently collapsing in his locked office beside a bloodied bronze brain model.
initialTheory: The office appears to be the murder scene. The first working theory is that Simon was struck around 9:16 PM after locking himself inside.

briefing.setting: The Harlow Institute, a private behavioral neurology institute hosting its annual donor gala.
briefing.misleadingWeapon: A bronze brain model found near the body with blood on it.
publicFacts: Simon was seen alive after the gala speech.
His office door was locked from the inside when he was found.
The bronze brain model carried visible blood transfer.
Several guests, staff, and family members had reasons to resent him.

suggestedHypotheses.title: Simon was killed in the office.
suggestedHypotheses.description: The locked door and bronze model make the office look like the primary scene.

suggestedHypotheses.title: The bronze model was the murder weapon.
suggestedHypotheses.description: The model has blood transfer, but the injury pattern needs to match.

suggestedHypotheses.title: Simon was injured before entering the office.
suggestedHypotheses.description: Medical and behavioral clues suggest a dangerous interval before collapse.

suggestedHypotheses.title: The Memory Arch caused the fatal injury.
suggestedHypotheses.description: The exhibit's missing protective edge and timing point away from the office.

suggestedHypotheses.title: The corridor accident was intentional.
suggestedHypotheses.description: A tool sign-out, fiber transfer, and access logs make sabotage plausible.

suggestedHypotheses.title: Someone created the false donor message.
suggestedHypotheses.description: The message redirected Simon into a vulnerable route.

suggestedHypotheses.title: A financial scandal motivated the attack.
suggestedHypotheses.description: Payment trails and private drafts suggest Simon had identified an internal problem.
